title: Reconstruction of Ukraine's economy could cost $588 billion over 10 years
description: Four years to the day after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, we take a look at the state of the country's economy. The World Bank estimates it will take $588 billion over the next 10 years to make up for damage and destruction incurred during the conflict. The housing, transport and energy sectors have seen the biggest losses. Also in this edition: Panama takes control of two key ports on the Panama Canal.

description: This Tuesday, February 24 marks four years since Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, forcing millions to flee their homes. title: 'They look like a toy': Ukrainian civilians under threat from ‘petal mines’
description: PFM-1 anti-personnel mines have become a weapon of choice in the war in Ukraine. Also known as "petal mines", these mines are used by both sides, and can be scattered by rockets, bombs, and, increasingly, drones. Designed to cause injury rather than kill outright, they are, however, particularly dangerous for civilians because they are hard to identify – and to defuse. We spoke to demining experts, who explained the challenges posed by these mine

Other countries too are re-examining deals with the US in light of the ruling.

title: 'Nervous times in Tehran' ahead of crunch US talks and strike threat

Tehran and Washington's threats came as both sides worked to reach a deal in indirect talks due to restart in Switzerland on Thursday. France 24's Reza Sayah has the latest from Tehran.

title: In Brussels, volunteers assemble drones to be sent to Ukraine
